name: Burn (Rust Deep Learning Framework)

performance.notes: Multiple backends: Wgpu (GPU), LibTorch, CUDA, Metal; kernel fusion; async execution

agent_readiness.notes: Modern deep learning framework written in Rust. Pluggable backends: Wgpu (portable GPU via WebGPU), LibTorch (PyTorch backend), CUDA, Metal (Apple Silicon), NdArray (CPU), Candle. Automatic differentiation. Static and dynamic computational graphs. Module system similar to PyTorch nn.Module. Model import from ONNX and PyTorch weights. Compiles to WASM for browser inference. Growing rapidly — serious PyTorch alternative for Rust.

gotchas: Rapidly evolving API — breaking changes between versions
Smaller ecosystem than PyTorch (fewer pretrained models)
best_when: Deep learning in Rust production services
Cross-platform GPU ML without CUDA dependency (Wgpu backend)
Apple Silicon ML with Metal backend
avoid_when: When large pretrained model zoo availability matters
summary: Burn is a modern Rust deep learning framework with pluggable backends (Wgpu, CUDA, Metal, LibTorch, NdArray). Supports automatic differentiation, ONNX import, and WASM compilation for browser inference. PyTorch-like module API. Best for Rust services needing deep learning without Python, or cross-platform GPU inference. Still maturing but the most serious Python DL alternative in Rust.
